DEFENDANT CATHOLIC CHARITIES CYO OF THE ARCHDIOCESE OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR LEAVE TO To File Cross-Complaint For Indemnity is GRANTED on the conditions that a) no later than September 29, 2017 Catholic Charities serves verified code-complaint responses without any objections other than privilege to the deem-served discovery that it state all facts in support of each of its cross-claims, identify and provide contact information for each person who has knowledge of any of those facts and state what facts each person knows, and identify and produce all documents that refer or memorialize those facts and b) Catholic Charities not oppose any request for a trial continuance by cross-defendant Betty Shelton that does not exceed six months. California's policy liberally allowing amendments to pleadings strongly supports granting this motion. The conditions imposed on granting the motion eliminate any legal prejudice to Ms. Shelton. Catholic Charities has acted diligently in bringing this motion. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim and that complies with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Not Reported. (D302)
